task_completion.description: Perform an advanced search on the NSF Award Search portal for active Quantum Computing grants in Massachusetts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox browser showing the NSF Award Search page (or results page) with 'Quantum Computing' in the keyword field, 'Massachusetts' or 'MA' in the state field, and the 'Active' status checked.

task_completion.description: Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'NSF Quantum Grants'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox Bookmarks Toolbar, Bookmarks Menu, or Library window showing a folder explicitly named 'NSF Quantum Grants'.

task_completion.description: Bookmark 5 distinct NSF award pages into the created folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'NSF Quantum Grants' bookmark folder shown open/expanded, containing exactly 5 distinct bookmarks that link to NSF award pages.

task_completion.description: Compile the details of the 5 awards into a JSON format with specific fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying JSON content that includes an array of 5 objects, each containing the keys: 'award_number', 'title', 'organization', 'start_date', and 'amount'.

task_completion.description: Save the compiled JSON file to ~/Documents/nsf_grant_analysis.json.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor title bar, save dialog, or terminal command confirming the file is saved to '/home/ga/Documents/nsf_grant_analysis.json' or '~/Documents/nsf_grant_analysis.json'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox GUI to perform the search and create bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Firefox browser open and being interacted with, rather than the agent relying solely on terminal commands (like curl or python requests) to bypass GUI bookmarking.

integrity.description: The extracted JSON data comes from genuine NSF search results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON file contains realistic 7-digit award numbers and actual grant titles/organizations matching the search criteria, rather than placeholder, hallucinated, or hardcoded mock data.

integrity.description: The agent bookmarked and extracted 5 distinct awards.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 5 bookmarks in the folder and the 5 entries in the JSON file should have unique award numbers and titles, not just the same award duplicated 5 times.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target URL is https://www.nsf.gov/awardsearch/. The search must use Keyword 'Quantum Computing', State 'Massachusetts' (MA), and Status 'Active'. The agent must bookmark exactly 5 distinct grants in a Firefox folder named 'NSF Quantum Grants'. The extracted JSON file must be saved at /home/ga/Documents/nsf_grant_analysis.json and contain 5 entries with 7-digit award numbers and the fields: award_number, title, organization, start_date, and amount.
